title: Does Anyone Else Hate These Supposed New Elements
display_title: Does Anyone Else Hate These Supposed New Elements
wikitext:
[]: Does Anyone Else Hate These Supposed New Elements...?             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Fri Jul 19, 2013 1:03 pm Well, long time no see. It's been a while since I last posted here. Lately I've just been stalking the forums looking at people's theories and how sound they are. While there are a lot of theories I like and I think they could hold true based off of what we see, there are also other theories that I'm not to fond of but can still be true...and those things are the brainwashing concept and the fact that QRei can use telepathy. First, let's start with the brainwashing. People have theorized that brainwashing exists in NTE, as Gendo and Fuytsuki talked about it in the first movie, with Fuytuski suggesting brainwashing Shinji into piloting Eva Unit 01. And then there is another mention of supposed brainwashing in Q when Fuytuski was talking to Shinji about how his memory's were erased the day his mother went into Unit-01. Okay, technically that isn't so much brainwashing as more of erasing memory, but still. The reason why I don't like this element is because I feel this kind of goes against something NTE is trying to tell us. Basically, it's the fact that the character's are forced to do something, and instead not doing what they want out of their own free will. Shinji was responsible for initiating 3rd Impact right? Would that scenario have been like if he was brainwashed into doing it? It would obviously go against the fact that Shinji himself did Near Third-Impact, and the events of Q would be drastically different(assuming that Shinji is responsible for NTI, or how much involvement he was in.) Basically it's like that element forces Shinji to advance the plot, instead of the character itself to move the plot along. Now that I really think about though, I don't have much a problem with QRei being telepathic. I would be fine with that. But the brainwashing and memory erasing, not so much. What do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 19, 2013 2:13 pm Bear in mind the brainwashing thing is really just a theory like sequel and timeloop theory, based on ambiguous language in a single scene. I imagine if Shinji was being brainwashed he'd of had a reaction when Fuyutsuki said it straight up to his face.

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Fri Jul 19, 2013 4:26 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Bear in mind the brainwashing thing is really just a theory like sequel and timeloop theory, based on ambiguous language in a single scene. I imagine if Shinji was being brainwashed he'd of had a reaction when Fuyutsuki said it straight up to his face.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 19, 2013 4:39 pm Yes, Kozo said that in 1.0, as well as he has been perfectly clear to Shinji that someone erased his memories of the Contact Experiment. But I think that any shenanigans with his brain stopped with that memory swap : like you said, if it's revealed that most of Shinji's action through the three movies are the product of a mental programming, that would render the theme of surpassing ones flaws and taking responsibilities for one actions completely moot, as well a destroy any character development Shinji seemingly had during the Rebuild. But it was indeed a last resort solution Gendo and Fuyutsuki though of, makes you realize just how far these two can do, as well as how much dirtier Fuyutsuki's hands are compared to NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Fri Jul 19, 2013 5:56 pm ElMariachi wrote: Yes, Kozo said that in 1.0, as well as he has been perfectly clear to Shinji that someone erased his memories of the Contact Experiment. But I think that any shenanigans with his brain stopped with that memory swap : like you said, if it's revealed that most of Shinji's action through the three movies are the product of a mental programming, that would render the theme of surpassing ones flaws and taking responsibilities for one actions completely moot, as well a destroy any character development Shinji seemingly had during the Rebuild. But it was indeed a last resort solution Gendo and Fuyutsuki though of, makes you realize just how far these two can do, as well as how much dirtier Fuyutsuki's hands are compared to NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Jul 20, 2013 6:14 am I think it's clearly implied in 3.0 that whatever happened that left the world that lifeless red rock was not planned by anyone, be it Gendo or SEELE. Fuyutsuki is still following Gendo because since the beginning he seems to be far more implicated than in NGE, and after everything they have done, he probably can't bring himself to let go, if only to save Yui, exactly like Gendo. Anyway, back to the memory swap thing, in NGE Shinji didn't have many memories of the Contact Experiment, and only remembered it while within EVA-01 after Zeruel(or was it during the Leliel fight?), but in the end it didn't had a big importance until episode 24 where Ritsuko's revelations about Rei's true nature made him connect the dot between Rei, Yui and EVA-01. So the fact that someone decided to erase his memories means that something else was at stake back them. But even then it's just a tiny part of Shinji's back story, potentially important plot wise, but character wise he was far more defined by Gendo's abandonment and the years he lived alone with his teacher.

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Sat Jul 20, 2013 9:18 am ElMariachi wrote: I think it's clearly implied in 3.0 that whatever happened that left the world that lifeless red rock was not planned by anyone, be it Gendo or SEELE. Fuyutsuki is still following Gendo because since the beginning he seems to be far more implicated than in NGE, and after everything they have done, he probably can't bring himself to let go, if only to save Yui, exactly like Gendo. Anyway, back to the memory swap thing, in NGE Shinji didn't have many memories of the Contact Experiment, and only remembered it while within EVA-01 after Zeruel(or was it during the Leliel fight?), but in the end it didn't had a big importance until episode 24 where Ritsuko's revelations about Rei's true nature made him connect the dot between Rei, Yui and EVA-01. So the fact that someone decided to erase his memories means that something else was at stake back them. But even then it's just a tiny part of Shinji's back story, potentially important plot wise, but character wise he was far more defined by Gendo's abandonment and the years he lived alone with his teacher.

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Sat Jul 20, 2013 11:42 pm I don't understand. Brainwashing isn't a "new element" in the series, it was an offhand, vague comment by Fuyutsuki. If they introduced a whole new mechanic of the Eva, or a third species as well as the Angels, or something like that, that would be a "new element' you could hate. This just seems weird and nitpicky.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jul 21, 2013 1:03 am Th3Marauder wrote: I don't understand. Brainwashing isn't a "new element" in the series, it was an offhand, vague comment by Fuyutsuki. If they introduced a whole new mechanic of the Eva, or a third species as well as the Angels, or something like that, that would be a "new element' you could hate.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 21, 2013 3:10 am As we look back at the 2.0 hate threads of yore, the answer to the topic title is a clear "Yes, but OP's list doesn't cover the new elements that people actually took dislike to."
